channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🦠 WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda a Global Health Emergency
item.description: The World Health Organization declared an Ebola outbreak caused by the Bundibugyo virus in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern, signaling the severity of the spread.

item.title: 🇻🇪 Venezuelan Official Alex Saab, Maduro Ally, Deported to US
item.description: Venezuelan official Alex Saab, a Colombian-Venezuelan businessman and ally of former President Nicolas Maduro, was deported to the United States in a significant development involving Venezuela-US relations.

item.title: 🇰🇷 Samsung Electronics Chairman Apologizes Amid Labor Tensions
item.description: Samsung Electronics Chairman Jay Y. Lee apologized to customers and the public over the company wage dispute as the union prepared to resume pay talks, highlighting labor tensions at South Korea largest conglomerate.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Threatens Primary Challenge Against Republican Boebert
item.description: President Donald Trump threatened a primary challenge against hardline conservative Representative Lauren Boebert after she campaigned for fellow Republican Thomas Massie, signaling ongoing intra-party tensions.

item.title: ⛽ Rising Diesel Costs from Iran War Strain US School Budgets
item.description: Soaring diesel prices since the onset of the Iran war are draining already tight U.S. school district budgets, forcing some to draw from emergency financial reserves to cover transportation costs.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇺🇦 France to develop ballistic missile defense with Ukraine
item.description: President Zelensky announced France's readiness to develop ballistic missile defense capabilities with Ukraine, calling it an "important step" in strengthening capabilities against Russian attacks.

item.title: 🇲🇩🇷🇺 Putin simplifies Russian citizenship for Transnistria residents
item.description: Moldova's President Maia Sandu warned that Putin's decree simplifying citizenship for Transnistria residents is likely a recruitment tool to send more people to fight in Ukraine.

item.title: 🇮🇷🛢️ Iran to announce Hormuz traffic management mechanism
item.description: Iran is preparing a new mechanism to regulate shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, including designated routes and service fees, with European countries reportedly negotiating passage permissions.

item.title: 🇯🇵🌏 Japan to bolster sea lane defense with Southeast Asia info-sharing
item.description: Japan announced plans to strengthen sea lane defense through information sharing with Southeast Asian countries, amid growing regional tensions and supply chain concerns.

item.title: 🚀 BlackRock weighs $5-10 billion investment in SpaceX IPO
item.description: BlackRock has discussed investing $5-10 billion in SpaceX's initial public offering next month, the Information reported, marking one of the largest potential IPO investments by the asset management giant.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia captures two villages in Ukraine's Kharkiv region
item.description: Russian troops have taken control of the settlements of Borova and Kutkivka in Ukraine's Kharkiv region, according to Russia's RIA state news agency citing the Defence Ministry.

item.title: 📈 Iran's stock market to reopen Tuesday after war suspension
item.description: Iran will reopen its stock market on Tuesday after a suspension during the conflict with the US and Israel, IRNA news agency cited a senior official as saying, as the country begins normalizing economic activity.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canada deepens Arctic defense ties with Nordics amid US uncertainty
item.description: Canada is strengthening defense ties with Nordic countries as Prime Minister Mark Carney builds alliances among "middle powers" in a world where the United States is considered a less reliable partner.

item.title: ⚖️ US charges Iraqi man in Iran-linked terror plots against Jews
item.description: An Iraqi national has been arrested and brought to the US to face terrorism charges for involvement in multiple Iran-backed plots targeting US interests in Europe and Jewish communities.

item.title: 🌍 ISIS Deputy Leader Killed in US-Nigeria Joint Operation
item.description: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, the second-in-command of ISIS globally, was killed in a joint US-Nigerian military operation in northeastern Nigeria. President Trump and Nigerian President Tinubu confirmed the operation, marking a significant blow to the terrorist organization.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Tens of Thousands Protest in London as Rival Rallies Face Off
item.description: London deployed thousands of police officers to manage rival protests: a far-right "Unite the Kingdom" rally and a pro-Palestinian Nakba Day march. At least 11 people were arrested as authorities worked to keep the opposing groups separated.

item.title: 🇮🇱⚔️ IDF Infantry Commander Killed by Hezbollah Drone in Southern Lebanon
item.description: Captain Maoz Israel Recanati, 24, was killed by a Hezbollah drone attack in southern Lebanon. He was set to marry next month. The IDF struck over 100 targets in response.

item.title: 🇵🇸🏛️ Jerusalem Governorate Warns of Israeli Plan to Seize Properties Near Al-Aqsa
item.description: Palestinian authorities warn that Israel plans to approve seizure of Palestinian properties in the Bab al-Silsila neighborhood near Al-Aqsa Mosque on Sunday.

item.title: 🇭🇰🇺🇸 Trump Not Optimistic on Jimmy Lai Release After Raising Case With Xi
item.description: US President Trump says he raised the case of imprisoned Hong Kong media tycoon Jimmy Lai with Chinese President Xi, but is not optimistic about his release. Lai was sentenced to 20 years under Hong Kong national security law.

item.title: 🇱🇧⚠️ Hezbollah Urges Lebanon to Abandon Peace Illusions With Israel
item.description: Hezbollah warned Lebanese authorities against direct negotiations with Israel as Washington pushes forward with a new round of talks between Beirut and Tel Aviv.

item.title: 🇸🇱🇺🇸✈️ Sierra Leone to Accept Hundreds of West Africans Deported from US
item.description: Sierra Leone agreed to take in hundreds of West African migrants being deported by the United States, its foreign minister told Reuters. The deal is part of the Trump administration's broader effort to accelerate removals amid heightened immigration enforcement.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇺🇸🛡️ Taiwan Presses US Arms Sales Amid Uncertainty Over Future Deals
item.description: Taiwan urged the United States to continue arms sales as a cornerstone of regional peace after President Trump said he had not decided on future weapons transfers. The comments come amid broader uncertainty about US defense commitments in the Indo-Pacific.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚽🌍 FIFA Officials Meet Iranian FA to Discuss World Cup Participation
item.description: FIFA Secretary-General Mattias Grafstrom held constructive talks with Iran's Football Federation in Istanbul, offering reassurance about the country's participation in this year's World Cup amid concerns about Iran's status following recent regional conflicts.

item.title: 🇱🇻🏛️ Latvian President Proposes Opposition Lawmaker as Next Prime Minister
item.description: Latvian President Edgars Rinkevics nominated opposition lawmaker Andris Kulbergs as the next prime minister after Evika Silina's resignation. The move comes as Baltic states strengthen defense ties amid regional security concerns.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️⚖️ Trump-Targeted Senator Cassidy Faces Primary Challenge in Louisiana
item.description: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy, targeted by Trump for retribution, faces a critical primary election in Louisiana against Trump-backed and other rivals. The race is seen as a test of Trump's influence over GOP incumbents.
